pinterest_cluster_labels: Japandi neutral dining set with woven pendant
Minimal plant-forward living room with low wood table

vibe_report.summary: A calm, warm Japandi interior tuned for a young family: low-profile furniture, warm neutrals, tactile natural materials, and a small dedicated work corner. Prioritize organic shapes, layered natural textures, and biophilic accents while keeping sightlines uncluttered for an open-plan flow between living, dining, and kitchen.
vibe_report.palette_rationale: Warm neutral base (pale oat, soft beige) with mid-tone warm wood accents and deeper earthen anchors. Use subtle contrast rather than saturated color to preserve a calm, cohesive look across connected spaces.
vibe_report.lighting_mood: Soft ambient daylight through bay windows and a single exterior window, supplemented by warm practicals (woven pendants, table lamps). Directional daylight should create gentle shadows and sculpt surfaces—no artificial backlighting that contradicts window positions.
materials: Blackened brass / dark metal (small hardware and lamp stems)
furniture_language: round tables with slim tapered legs
integrated, compact standing desk with slim tabletop
styling_rules: Limit main palette to 3 warm neutrals + 1 deep anchor; repeat same tones across sightlines.
Layer 2–3 textures per sightline (e.g., linen sofa + wool rug + ceramic vase).
Use single sculptural pendant above dining table; keep tabletop minimal (one vase or simple place setting).
Place greenery as vertical accents (1–2 medium plants visible per primary sightline).
Work corner should feel integrated and minimal: standing_desk + narrow bookshelf, no office clutter visible from living/dining.
avoid: glossy or lacquered finishes that read as modern/reflective
bright saturated colors or patterned upholstery
large visual clutter on open shelves

room_guidance.headline: Low, plant-forward family living — calm and open
room_guidance.guidance: Compose a low, inviting seating area anchored by the shared rug and sofa. Keep coffee table minimal with 1–2 ceramic vessels. Place a medium potted plant in the corner near the bay window to create vertical green interest that reads from the dining area. Ensure the shared dining pendant remains visible in the background to tie sightlines together.
must_include: shared: low-profile 3-seater sofa — pale oat linen upholstery (#EDE6DE), light oak recessed base, low back
shared: layered neutral flatweave rug — natural wool & jute blend, off-white ground (#EDE6DE) with subtle oatmeal border (#D2BFA3), low pile
shared: organic-shaped low coffee table — medium walnut veneer, elliptical form, matte lacquer
one medium potted ficus lyrata in matte terracotta pot (#8A6E52)
2–3 dark ceramic vessels (small) on coffee table
must_avoid: large TV or entertainment units visible in primary shot

room_guidance.headline: Compact round dining with woven pendant focal
room_guidance.guidance: Center the round dining table under the oversized woven pendant. Use 4 slim-profile chairs in textured neutral upholstery. Provide minimal place settings and a single sculptural hand-thrown vase. Keep the window area uncluttered to emphasize daylight and view.
must_include: shared: round light oak dining table — light oak veneer, ~48in diameter, slim tapered legs, matte oiled finish
shared: oversized woven rattan pendant — natural seagrass dome, warm honey tone (#B58E61)
chairs with textured neutral upholstery (no visible pattern)
simple ceramic vase (hand-thrown)

room_guidance.headline: Quiet, minimal kitchen edge — functional & visible
room_guidance.guidance: Keep counters mostly clear; stage a small ceramic bowl and a single potted herb near the window. If a small breakfast table or stool is visible, keep it in the same light oak tone as dining. Ensure all visible surfaces reference the same warm wood and neutral tones as the dining/living sightlines.
must_include: visible from sightline: shared: round light oak dining table — light oak veneer, ~48in diameter, slim tapered legs, matte oiled finish (if table is visible)
small ceramic bowl and single potted herb on counter
matte black metal task faucet or hardware as subtle anchor

room_guidance.headline: Calm, low-profile bedroom with cozy work corner
room_guidance.guidance: Primary composition: low bed with pale linen bedding, minimal nightstand. Add a compact standing_desk and narrow bookshelf as a small work corner near the window—desk should be maple/light oak and slim profile. Keep textiles warm and minimal and place one small plant on the bookshelf for continuity.
must_include: low bed with pale oat linen bedding (#EDE6DE)
compact standing_desk (maple/light oak slim profile) and narrow bookshelf (light oak open shelving)
one small potted plant on bookshelf
one bedside task lamp in blackened brass
must_avoid: large, heavy desks that dominate the room
office monitors or visible cables in main bedroom shot

room_guidance.headline: Spa-like micro-bath with warm materials
room_guidance.guidance: Keep styling minimal: folded towels, a single ceramic soap dispenser, and a small terracotta planter by the frosted window. Use warm wood or wood-effect vanity and matte black fixtures to connect back to the apartment palette.
must_include: matte wood vanity (light oak tone)
matte black fixtures
single terracotta planter near frosted window
folded neutral towels

warnings: Kitchen is small — avoid staging large furniture or an island that contradicts measured layout.
Ensure standing_desk and bookshelf in Dream do not block egress or appear oversized given 12.5' x 11.58' bedroom.
Cross-room object specs must remain identical in every room where they are visible.

global_style_notes: Preserve all architectural geometry and verticals; derive all lighting from visible windows and practical fixtures.
Keep exposure balanced with slightly raised interior midtones and controlled, directional shadows for editorial depth.
Limit decorative objects per sightline to 3 key pieces to maintain uncluttered Japandi aesthetic.
creative_spec.negative_prompt: no color inconsistency between rooms, no mismatched furniture across sightlines, blur, distort, low quality, warped architecture, flicker, unstable camera, unrealistic lighting, muddy shadows, layout drift

pins.title: Japandi Dining Room with Neutral Decor
pins.description: A Japandi dining room with neutral décor blends Japanese simplicity and Scandinavian warmth. Soft beige, warm wood, and gentle earth tones create a calm, inviting space. Clean lines, natural materials, and minimal furnishings keep the room feeling open and uncluttered, while subtle textures like linen, ceramic, and light wood add quiet elegance and comfort.
pins.cluster_label: Japandi neutral dining set with woven pendant

pins.title: Japandi Living Room: Calm Minimal Decor, Natural Wood & Indoor Plants
pins.description: Calm Japandi living room inspiration with a light neutral sofa, low wooden coffee table, soft textured rug, and plenty of indoor plants for a serene, balanced space. Minimal wall art, natural materials, and clean lines create an airy, soothing aesthetic—perfect for Japanese style interiors, minimalist living room ideas, and warm neutral home decor. Save for Japandi decor and plant-filled living room styling.
pins.cluster_label: Minimal plant-forward living room with low wood table

cluster_summary.label: Japandi neutral dining set with woven pendant
cluster_summary.visual_notes: Round light-oak dining table, slim tapered legs, textured neutral upholstery, oversized woven pendant as focal point, ceramic tableware and hand-thrown vases. Flooring and paneling in pale oak unify the composition—stage with a single sculptural vase, linen place settings, and warm-toned tabletop ceramics.

cluster_summary.label: Low-floor Japanese reading/tea nook
cluster_summary.visual_notes: Intimate corner with floor cushions and low coffee tray, bamboo/shōji screening, layered natural-fiber rugs and woven poufs. Warm paper/lantern lighting creates deep amber glow—use soft cushions, round woven floor mats, low tray table and minimal potted greenery to recreate cozy scale.

cluster_summary.label: Minimal plant-forward living room with low wood table
cluster_summary.visual_notes: Low, neutral sofa on wooden base, organic-shaped low coffee table, vertical wood slat accent and sparse framed art. Emphasize neutral textiles, textured rug, and multiple medium potted plants for vertical green accents—keep decor minimal: 2–3 dark ceramic vessels and one sculptural plant grouping.
